title: pseudorchis albida subsp. straminea
intro: Pseudorchis albida subsp. straminea, also known as the pale wintergreen or pale pseudorchis, is a rare and threatened species of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Pseudorchis albida subsp. straminea include its potential to help with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to act as a natural antihistamine. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
